mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which overwhelms corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, aggressive federal funds rate hikes force equity risk premiums higher, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of high-inflation tightening episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate revenue streams, which directly supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms weakening aggregate demand and labor market slack, prompting equity de-risking and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and declining real estate starts signals a liquidity squeeze in the credit-dependent housing sector, which transmits to broader equity markets and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain bottlenecks where industrial production growth outpaces labor force participation growth, corporate margins expand due to pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month MA growth rate > PAYEMS 3-month MA growth rate
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth consistently exceeds PAYEMS growth for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for input cost indices.
